post.summary: Log returns budget stats for logs.
Overall returns budeget stats.
Trace returns budget stats for traces.

GetFilter returns the current filter setting of the given filter type.
If  an update is in progress, this will return with an error code `unavailable`.

UpdateFilter attempts to update filter setting of the given filter type.
If an update is in progress, this will immediately return with an error code `unavailable`.
Otherwise, this will continuously give update until the update is complete or errored.
TimeframeStatus returns timeframe status for each timeframe.
SaveArgoConnection saves the argo connection details.
TestConnection uses the provided request data to test the argo connection.
CreateConnection creates a connection and deploys it
CreateConnectionValidatorRun creates a new validator run for the given connection. Will create a new validator run if one already exists.
DeleteConnection removes an existing connection and its associated resources
DeleteConnectionValidator deletes connection validator resources

GenerateManifests generates the manifest base on the given input and this will returns
the compressed zip file containing the manifests as a stream of raw bytes.
Note: To create a download link in the FE, the stream of file content data should be stored
in a typed blob and then https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/URL/createObjectURL_static
should be used.
GetConnection gets details about a connection
GetConnectionStatus gets the status of a connection based on dataflow and validation metrics
GetConnectionValidatorRuns gets the validator runs that exist in the validation dataset
GetConnections gets all existing connection names
GetDatadogIntegrations returns list of datadog integration names.

SaveDatadogIntegration saves the given datadog integration.
If the integration already exists, this will override the saved data with the one provided.
GetInstallStatus creates a response stream with mdai install status updates.
InstallMDAIHub initiates installing the mdai smart hub with the provided request data.

received.description:
received represents the total amount of data recieved.
For log, the unit is in GB; For trace, the unit is in mm Events.

sent.description:
sent represents the total amount of data send.
For log, the unit is in GB; For trace, the unit is in mm Events.

filtered.description:
filtered represents the difference between sent and recieved.
For log, the unit is in GB; For trace, the unit is in mm Events.

costRate.description:
cost_rate contains the per GB (for log) or the per mm Events (for traces) cost rate.
Remember to round to the nearest two decimal places.

pct.description:
pct contains the percentage of total overall cost (e.g., 1.2345%).
Remember to round to the nearest two decimal places.

cost.description:
cost contains the total cost.
Remember to round to the nearest two decimal places.

TimeframeStatusResponseCode.description:
Code represents the status code of a given timeframe.

 - CODE_OK: CODE_OK means user is allow to select the given timeframe.
 - CODE_NO_DATA: CODE_NO_DATA means connection is not set, so no data to show.
 - CODE_NOT_ENOUGH: CODE_NOT_ENOUGH means there are not enough data to show for the given timeframe.

sent.description: sent represents the amount of data send (in GB).

pct.description:
pct contains the percentage of total log cost (e.g., 1.2345%).
Remember to round to the nearest two decimal places.

cost.description:
cost contains the total cost.
Remember to round to the nearest two decimal places.

cost contains the total cost for log and trace.
Remember to round to the nearest two decimal places.

log.description: log contains overall metric data for logs.

trace.description: trace contains overall metric data for traces.

namespace.title: namespace that the connection is installed in/will be installed into

connectionName.title: connection_name is the name of the connection

v1alphaConnectionScope.title: ConnectionScope contains identifiers for a connection

telemetryTypes.description: telemetry_types is used to generate the manifests.

deployment.title: deployment contains information required for deploying the connection

destinations.title: destinations are where telemetry from this connection is sent and options needed to send it there

v1alphaCreateConnectionRequest.title: CreateConnectionRequest contains options for creating a connection

v1alphaCreateConnectionValidatorRunReque.title: CreateConnectionValidatorRunRequest has options for creating a connection validator

validatorRunId.title: validator_run_id is the created validator_run_id for the new validator run

v1alphaCreateConnectionValidatorRunRespo.title: CreateConnectionValidatorRunResponse contains a response for validator creation

connectionName.title: connection_name is the name of the connection to delete

v1alphaDeleteConnectionValidatorRequest.title: DeleteConnectionValidatorRequest contains options for deleting a connection validator

type.title: type indicates what type of deployment mechanism/system to use

integrationName.title: integration_name is the name of the previously created deployment integration to use

v1alphaDeployment.title: Deployment is information needed to deploy a connection

includeErr.description: include_err indcates if errors should be included.

v1alphaFilterType.description: FilterType represents the type of filters supported.

format.description: format contains output format of the manifests.

deploymentType.description: deployment_type use to tell the type of manifest to generate.

telemetryTypes.description: telemetry_types is used to generate the manifests.

mdaiVersion.description: mdai_version is the mdai hub version to use for app install.

data.description: data contains a chunk of the file content as raw bytes.

total.description: total is the total number of bytes for current file.

type.title: type is the MIME type of the file. (e.g., zip)

connectionName.title: connection_name is the name of the connection

v1alphaGetConnectionRequest.title: GetConnectionRequest are fields required to fetch a connection

telemetryTypes.title: telemetry_types are the MLT telemetry types for this connection

destinations.title: destinations are where telemetry for this connection flows to

v1alphaGetConnectionResponse.title: GetConnectionResponse has details on a connection

validatorRunId.description: validator_run_id is the Validator Run ID to get validation metrics for. Omitting this will yield empty/false validation results in the response.

v1alphaGetConnectionStatusRequest.title: GetConnectionStatusRequest contains options for getting a connections validator run results

receivingData.title: receiving_data indicates if the Connection's Collector is receiving telemetry for its configured Telemetry types

sendingData.title: sending_data indicates if the Connection's Collector is sending telemetry for its configured Telemetry types

dataIntegrity.title: sending_data indicates if the data coming to the Collector fulfills validator parity or policy requirements

clientsConnected.title: clients_connected indicates if the Connection's Collector is connected for its configured Telemetry types

validationResults.title: validation_results is a map of granular validation details per data type (MLT)

v1alphaGetConnectionValidatorRunIdsReque.title: GetConnectionValidatorRunIdsRequest is a request to get all available connection validator runs

validatorRunIds.title: validator_run_ids is the list of validator_run_ids currently available in the metrics set

v1alphaGetConnectionValidatorRunIdsRespo.title: GetConnectionValidatorRunIdsResponse provides all the validator run ids for a connection

connectionNames.title: connection_names are the connection names found in the namespace

v1alphaGetConnectionsResponse.title: GetConnectionsResponse contains a list of all the connections in a namespace

names.title: names contains the list of datadog integration names

type.description: type contains the type of the filter to retrieve.

namespace.description: namespace is the kubernetes namespace to install into.

connectionName.title: connection_name is the name of this connection and its resources in argo/k8s

connectionName.description: connection_name is the name of the octant connection to check install status for.

installStatus.description: install_status is the status of the installation.

details.description: details will contain any necessary details for states that have extra information.

namespace.description: namespace is the kubernetes namespace to install into.

connectionName.description: connection_name is the named octant connection configuration to use for install.

mdaiVersion.description: mdai_version is the mdai hub version to use for install.

v1alphaIntegrationType.description:
- INTEGRATION_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: INTEGRATION_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED is unsupported and should not be used
 - INTEGRATION_TYPE_DATADOG: INTEGRATION_TYPE_DATADOG is for datadog telemetry destinations
v1alphaIntegrationType.title: IntegrationType is the type of integration for destinations or deployments

timeframe.description: timeframe tells the server timeframe of the data to retrieve.

size.description: size limits number of services to retrieve.

pageToken.description: page_token (pagination) used to grab next page of data.

search.description: search is used to filter the data so that only services with name containing the given search term will be returned.

namespace.description: namespace is the kubernetes namespace to install into.

connectionName.title: connection_name is the name of this connection and its resources in argo/k8s

timeframe.description: timeframe tells the server timeframe of the data to retrieve.

namespace.description: namespace is the kubernetes namespace to install into.

name.title: name is the name of the resource (pod name, etc.)

message.description: message describes the state of the resource.

argoEndpoint.description: argo_endpoint is the endpoint to the argo cd cluster to communicate with.

argoAccountToken.description: argo_account_token is the generated token for the account to use for communicating with the argo cd API.

name.title: name contains name of the argocd integration to save
name contains name of the datadog integration to save

name.description: name is the trace root span name.

invocations.description: invocations contains the number of invocations.

cost.description:
cost contains the total cost.
Remember to round to the nearest two decimal places.

type.title: type is the integration type for this integration

integrationName.title: integration_name is the name of a previously created integration with information needed to send telemetry

v1alphaTelemetryDestination.title: TelemetryDestination is information for an integration to send telemetry to from a connection

argoEndpoint.description: argo_endpoint is the endpoint to the argo cd cluster to communicate with.

argoAccountToken.description: argo_account_token is the generated token for the account to use for communicating with the argo cd API.

success.description: success indicates whether the operation was succesful or not.

v1alphaTimeframe.description:
Timeframe represents possible timeframes.

 - TIMEFRAME_24HR: TIMEFRAME_24HR means the last 24 hours.
 - TIMEFRAME_MTD: TIMEFRAME_MTD means month to date.
 - TIMEFRAME_LM: TIMEFRAME_LM means last month.

connectionName.title: connection_name is the name of this connection and its resources in argo/k8s

namespace.description: namespace is the kubernetes namespace to install into.

statuses.description:
statuses contains status of each timeframe.
Note: this array will not contain duplicate timeframes.

trace.description:
trace will contain true if there are data for traces.
Otherwise, this will contain false.
Note: this containing true just means there are data,
and it doesn't necessarily mean there will be data
for the user's selected timeframe.

log.description:
log will contain true if there are data for logs.
Otherwise, this will contain false.
Note: this containing true just means there are data,
and it doesn't necessarily mean there will be data
for the user's selected timeframe.

status.description: status contains the status code of the timeframe.

timeframe.description: timeframe tells the server timeframe of the data to retrieve.

size.description: size limits number of trace root spans to retrieve.

pageToken.description: page_token (pagination) used to grab next page of data.

search.description: search is used to filter the data so that only trace root span with name containing the given search term will be returned.

namespace.description: namespace is the kubernetes namespace to install into.

connectionName.title: connection_name is the name of this connection and its resources in argo/k8s

data.description:
data contains the filter setting data to update.
`type` indicates which filter type should be updated.

namespace.description: namespace is the kubernetes namespace to install into.

connectionName.title: connection_name is the name of this connection and its resources in argo/k8s

v1alphaUpdateFilterResponseStatus.description:
- STATUS_VALUE_UPDATED: STATUS_VALUE_UPDATED means the data have been updated in MDAI gateway.
 - STATUS_WAIT_PROPAGATION: STATUS_WAIT_PROPAGATION means we are waiting for the collectors to restart with the new values.
 - STATUS_COMPLETED: STATUS_COMPLETED means collectors are all updated.

parity.title: parity indicates if the ingress attribute matches the attribute on egress

policy.title: parity indicates if the attribute fulfills the MDAI validator policy for this attribute

parity.title: parity indicates if the ingress telemetry payloads match the egress telemetry payloads

policy.title: parity indicates if the egress telemetry payloads fulfill the MDAI validator policy

attributes.title: attributes is a map of attributes to per-attribute parity/policy checks
